concepts.title: Equation of pair of tangents
concepts.conceptBody:
Equation of pair of tangents to an ellipse from an external point $\left( {{x}_{1}},{{y}_{1}} \right)$. 

Let the given ellipse be $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$

Let $\text{P}\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)$ be an external point.

$\left( \frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}-1 \right)\left( \frac{x_{1}^{2}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{y_{1}^{2}}{{{b}^{2}}}-1 \right)={{\left( \frac{{{x}_{1}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{y}_{1}}}{{{b}^{2}}}-1 \right)}^{2}}$

This can be written as \(\mathrm{SS}_1=\mathrm{T}^2\)

where $\text{S},{{\text{S}}_{1}}$ and $\text{T}$ have usual meanings

 Equation to the chord of contact of tangents drawn from a point $\left( {{x}_{1}},{{y}_{1}} \right)$ is 

\(\frac{x_1}{a^2}+\frac{y y_1}{b^2}=1\)

Statement II The condition on $a$ and $\mathrm{b}$ for which two distinct chords of the ellipse $\frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=$ 2 passing through $(a,-b)$ are bisected by the line $x+y=b$ is $a^2+6 a b-7 b^2\gt0$ Statement II Equation of chord of the ellipse $\frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$ whose mid-point $\left(x_1, y_1\right)$ is $T=S_1$
